title: Pataleeswarar Temple
tagline: Pataleeswarar Temple — spiritual town of Tamil Nadu

overview.short: Pataleeswarar Temple (பாடலீஸ்வரர் கோயில்) is a Hindu shrine dedicated to Shiva in the town of Thirupathiripuliyur, Cuddalore.
overview.description: Pataleeswarar Temple (பாடலீஸ்வரர் கோயில்) is a Hindu shrine dedicated to Shiva in the town of Thirupathiripuliyur, Cuddalore. It was constructed during the Pallava and Medieval Chola periods. The Saivite saint Appar is believed to have adopted Saivism at this temple. It is one of the shrines of the 275 Paadal Petra Sthalams.

topPlaces.name: Thirupathiripuliyur
topPlaces.description: Thirupathiripuliyur is a neighbourhood of Cuddalore, Tamil Nadu, India. It is named for the ancient Hindu temple located in the locale, which is named after the Pathiri tree and Puliyur, a tiger-legged saint who obtained absolution in the area.

topPlaces.name: Kudalur, Thanjavur
topPlaces.description: Kudalur is a village in the Thanjavur taluk of Thanjavur district, Tamil Nadu, India.

topPlaces.name: Cuddalore division
topPlaces.description: Cuddalore division is a revenue division in the Cuddalore district of Tamil Nadu, India.

topPlaces.name: Battle of Cuddalore (1758)
topPlaces.description: The Battle of Cuddalore took place on 29 April 1758 during the Seven Years' War near Cuddalore off the Carnatic coast of India, and was an indecisive battle between a British squadron under Vice-Admiral George Pocock and French squadron under Anne Antoine, Comte d'Aché. The British casualties were 29 killed and 89 wounded, while France lost 99 killed and 321 wounded.

topPlaces.name: Cuddalore block
topPlaces.description: Cuddalore block is a revenue block of Cuddalore district of the Indian state of Tamil Nadu. This revenue block consist of 51 panchayat villages.

topPlaces.name: Cuddalore
topPlaces.description: Cuddalore, also spelt as Kadalur ( ), is a heavy industries hub, port city, and headquarters of the Cuddalore District in the Indian state of Tamil Nadu. Situated 5.3 km south of Puducherry (union territory), the city was an important port city during the British Raj and is now famous for Pichavaram Mangrove Forest.

topPlaces.name: Cuddalore taluk
topPlaces.description: Cuddalore taluk is a taluk of Cuddalore district of the Indian state of Tamil Nadu. The headquarters of the taluk is the town of Cuddalore.

topPlaces.name: Siege of Cuddalore
topPlaces.description: The siege of Cuddalore was a siege attempt by British troops against a combined French and Mysorean garrison at the fortress of Cuddalore in the Second Anglo-Mysore War. The siege ended when news arrived of a preliminary peace treaty between France and Britain.

items.note: Thirupathiripuliyur is a neighbourhood of Cuddalore, Tamil Nadu, India. It is named for the ancient Hindu temple located in the locale, which is named after the Pathiri tree and Puliyur, a tiger-legged saint who obtained absolution in the area.
Kudalur is a village in the Thanjavur taluk of Thanjavur district, Tamil Nadu, India.
Cuddalore division is a revenue division in the Cuddalore district of Tamil Nadu, India.

The Battle of Cuddalore took place on 29 April 1758 during the Seven Years' War near Cuddalore off the Carnatic coast of India, and was an indecisive battle between a British squadron under Vice-Admiral George Pocock and French squadron under Anne Antoine, Comte d'Aché. The British casualties were 29 killed and 89 wounded, while France lost 99 killed and 321 wounded.
Cuddalore block is a revenue block of Cuddalore district of the Indian state of Tamil Nadu. This revenue block consist of 51 panchayat villages.

Cuddalore, also spelt as Kadalur ( ), is a heavy industries hub, port city, and headquarters of the Cuddalore District in the Indian state of Tamil Nadu. Situated 5.3 km south of Puducherry (union territory), the city was an important port city during the British Raj and is now famous for Pichavaram Mangrove Forest.
Cuddalore taluk is a taluk of Cuddalore district of the Indian state of Tamil Nadu. The headquarters of the taluk is the town of Cuddalore.

The siege of Cuddalore was a siege attempt by British troops against a combined French and Mysorean garrison at the fortress of Cuddalore in the Second Anglo-Mysore War. The siege ended when news arrived of a preliminary peace treaty between France and Britain.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Pataleeswarar Temple?
faq.a: The best time to visit Pataleeswarar Temple is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Pataleeswarar Temple?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Chennai International Airport (146 km) and the nearest railway station is Tiruchirappalli Junction (157 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Pataleeswarar Temple?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Pataleeswarar Temple at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Pataleeswarar Temple?
faq.a: Stays in Pataleeswarar Temple start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Pataleeswarar Temple?
faq.a: The top attractions are Thirupathiripuliyur, Kudalur, Thanjavur, Cuddalore division, Battle of Cuddalore (1758), Cuddalore block.
